params.name: Moenn Faucet
params.description:
On Sept 11, 1947 AD, the world's first single-handle mixing faucet was manufactured.
Early faucets used two handles to control water temperature, but they posed a scalding risk. A young man named Al Moen got the idea of using a single handle to solve this problem after being scalded himself. He invented the single-handle mixing faucet, which quickly gained popularity and received praise from users. Ten years later, Moen's invention was selected as one of the top 100 best-designed mass-produce …
